title: First real bridge evaluation baseline

input_bundle.extraction_notes: Real hidden-state extraction; raw prompts and model text outputs are not stored in shards.

interpretation: retrieval_top1=1.0 over two samples is a positive operational signal but statistically weak because sample_count=2.
cosine_diag_mean=0.1121 indicates weak absolute directional alignment.
diagonal_margin_mean=0.0404 indicates the correct target was only slightly closer than the best incorrect target.
norm_ratio_mean=0.2265 and energy_drift_mean=121.3654 indicate substantial target-space energy/norm mismatch.
This baseline suggests the bridge has early discriminative signal but needs larger bundles, more training, and energy/norm calibration.
known_limitations: The evaluation uses only two real latent pairs.
The checkpoint was trained for one completed CPU step in the recorded H0-003 run.
retrieval_top1 is not statistically meaningful at this sample count.
The evaluation is latent-space only and does not evaluate text-level semantic fidelity.
The result does not establish model-to-model alignment or production readiness.
data_policy: No generated eval outputs, checkpoints, shards, bundles, datasets, zips, caches, or run artifacts are committed.
scientific_claim_status: This PR registers the first real latent-space bridge evaluation baseline. It does not claim semantic transfer, text-level fidelity, model-to-model alignment, or production readiness.
